title: The Game of Kings
subtitle: the dice that moved a kingdom
goalMeaning: In the epic, a game of dice decided the fate of an empire. Bring your four home not by fortune alone, but by walking the razor's edge of dharma.

enter.text: A warrior enters the field. In the great epic, too, every player was drawn onto the board by a throw of dice.
Yudhishthira staked all on the cowries and lost a kingdom. Enter, but never forget the cost of the game.
The chariot rolls out from the centre. Let your aim be true.

castle.text: A fortress square. Even on the battlefield of Kurukshetra there were sanctuaries where no arrow could reach.
Krishna counsels patience. Hold your ground and let the reckless spend themselves against fate.
Here you are untouchable. Wisdom knows when to strike and when to stand still.

capture.text: You strike an opponent from the board, as warriors fell in the eighteen days of war. Victory and grief share one face.
The dice of the Kauravas sent the Pandavas into exile. Now your throw sends another back to the beginning.
Arjuna hesitated to strike his kin. To act, yet act without hatred — that is the harder war.

captured.text: Your warrior is cast back to the Charkoni, as the Pandavas were cast into thirteen years of exile. Endure, and return.
Fortune's wheel has turned against you. The epic teaches that even the righteous must suffer the fall.
Defeat is a teacher in disguise. Bhima remembered every humiliation and grew unbreakable.

home.text: A warrior completes the journey and reaches dharma's centre — the peace that no game of dice can gamble away.
After the war came the long walk to the mountains. One soul has finished that ascent.
This piece has crossed the whole field and come home righteous. Three chariots still ride.

win.text: All four are home. Where the epic's dice game brought ruin, your steady play has brought dharma back to the throne.
The kingdom is whole again. This is the victory Yudhishthira sought and finally, on the mountain, found.

journey.text: The chariot leaves its own lines and rides out onto the open field of Kurukshetra.
You enter contested ground. Here alliances are made and broken with every throw.
The tide of battle turns at the midpoint. From here the long road home begins.
The war behind you, the exile nearly served. Steadiness now, not haste.
Your own lines rise ahead — the last homeward march up the centre.
